title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Bartholomew and Inspector Bartlett reveal the resolution of the mystery regarding the "Seven O'Clock" killer. They expose that Cecil, previously dismissed as a "brainless idiot," is actually a ruthless criminal. To apprehend him, Bill Everly acted as a decoy. Cecil attempted to poison Everly with a drink laced with hydrochloric morphine, but Everly poured the liquid into a jar and feigned unconsciousness. Cecil then attempted to establish an alibi by driving to a golf course and visiting the Seven Dials Club, eventually hiding in a room to surprise his victims.

The conspiracy involved Lorel, a woman with a criminal past, and Jimmy, who assisted Cecil in moving the supposedly unconscious Everly. Their plan was to leave the victims together to be discovered at seven o'clock, but the scheme failed due to the intervention of Sexton and the presence of Eileen Bondel. Inspector Bartlett notes that Cecil is destined to be hanged, while Lorel's fate remains uncertain.

Following the conclusion of the investigation, Bondel informs her father, Lord Kettlemere, of her intention to marry Bill Everly. Lord Kettlemere, who discusses his plans to write memoirs with a ghostwriter, reacts with satisfaction to the news, noting that Everly will make an excellent partner for his upcoming autumn golf doubles competition.
